name: @radzor/chatbot-flow
description: Build conversational chatbot flows with nodes, conditions, and actions. Supports branching dialogues, context variables, fallback handling, and flow completion tracking.

inputs.name: flow
inputs.description: Chat flow definition with nodes, edges, and conditions.

inputs.name: fallbackMessage
inputs.default: I didn't understand that. Could you rephrase?
inputs.description: Default message when no matching transition is found.

inputs.name: maxHistory
inputs.description: Maximum number of messages to keep in conversation history.

outputs.name: botResponse
outputs.description: Response generated by the chatbot flow.

fields.name: options
fields.description: Quick-reply options for the user, if any.

fields.name: nodeId
fields.description: ID of the current flow node.

fields.name: isComplete
fields.description: Whether the flow has reached an end node.

events.name: onNodeReached
events.description: Fired when the conversation enters a new node.

events.name: onFlowComplete
events.description: Fired when the conversation reaches an end node.

events.name: onFallback
events.description: Fired when no transition matches the user's input.

actions.name: start

params.name: sessionId
params.description: Unique session identifier for the conversation.

actions.description: Start a new conversation session from the root node.

actions.name: processMessage
actions.description: Process a user message and return the bot's response.

actions.name: setContext
actions.description: Set a context variable in an active session.

actions.name: getHistory
actions.description: Get the conversation history for a session.

actions.name: reset
actions.description: Reset a session to the root node with fresh context.

connectsTo.description: Notify support team when chatbot flow completes
Escalate to LLM when no flow transition matches

llm.constraints: Sessions are in-memory; persist externally for production. Flow definitions are immutable after construction. Condition matchers are case-insensitive by default.
